Launched in 2005, Eye on the Market has remained one of the most widely respected sources of analysis in our industry. For two decades, Michael Cembalest, Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, has shared his views on markets, investments, economics and more with clients. To celebrate, we are delighted to share a special 20th anniversary retrospective edition containing 30 of his most memorable insights.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text: A weekend where innovation met elegance. At @theauroraconcours, together with @jpmorganprivatebank, moments unfolded like scenes from a modern Nordic dream, from curated conversations to sculptural speed and style.

Last Friday, U.S. equities reached new all-time highs, and we see the potential for further upside. While investors may not always feel comfortable buying when markets are experiencing new highs, history shows that investors getting in at all-time highs have actually ended up outperforming those investing at non-all-time highs. While this may sound counterintuitive, there’s a simple explanation. Historically, all-time highs beget more all-time highs, and by staying on the sidelines, investors risk missing out. The takeaway? Don’t let all-time highs steer you away from your plan and long-term investment goals.

This June, our LGBTQ+ Wealth Strategies team brought together clients, colleagues and business leaders for meaningful events across the country. From intimate dinners and forums to parades and conversations with inspiring executives, each gathering reflected our shared commitment to fostering belonging and building an inclusive future.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

As markets hover near all-time highs amid uncertainty, it’s essential to focus on the positives. A strong economy and robust corporate growth offer opportunities. Learn how a phased-in investment approach can help manage volatility and enhance your portfolio. #TopMarketTakeaways

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Our second year as the Global Banking and Finance partner at the 1000 Miglia was nothing short of extraordinary. 

The spectacle of the race, the thrill of the classic car simulators at our lounge, and exclusive cocktail reception at the picturesque Ca’ del Bosco offered our guests a taste of elegance and sophistication—adding a touch of luxury to the exhilarating experience. 

Tap the link in bio to relive the essence of this iconic event.

Serving Pasadena means working hand in hand with the individuals and families who shape this vibrant community. Our team of dedicated specialists partners with clients to develop tailored strategies to manage, grow and preserve their wealth, always with their goals at the center. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how our Pasadena team can help you.

Dealmaking is poised for a resurgence, driven by AI innovation, financial sector deregulation and private equity’s search for liquidity. Discover how these catalysts are shaping the M&A landscape, and explore opportunities to capitalize on these transformative trends. #TopMarketTakeaways

Tap the link in bio to learn more.
In celebration of #PrideMonth, we recognize and celebrate the diverse cultures, stories and contributions of our #LGBTQ+ colleagues across the firm.

Tensions between Iran and Israel have escalated, with Israel’s “Operation Rising Lion” targeting Iran’s nuclear sites and Iran retaliating with missile attacks. While the human cost is profound, the broader market impact may be limited. Historically, the returns of the S&P 500 Index around past conflicts suggest that geopolitical events often have short-lived effects. For investors, the underlying health of the economy usually matters more for longer-term market returns. Ultimately, staying invested in a diversified, goals-aligned portfolio has proven beneficial through countless geopolitical crises, wars, pandemics and recessions—and we believe this will continue to hold true. Stay informed, assess impacts, and focus on long-term strategies.

As Florida grows, so do the needs of the individuals and families who call it home. In Naples, Richard Grows is building a team of specialists who work in step with clients and with each other to deliver the full breadth of J.P. Morgan’s capabilities. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how we can help you get closer to your financial goals.

As Washington navigates deficit concerns, Section 899 and deregulation, we explore the potential impacts on markets and portfolios. Discover how these developments could shape investment opportunities in municipal bonds, international equities and U.S. financials. #TopMarketTakeaways

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Globally, long-term government bonds are seeing a rise in yields as demand decreases. But what does this mean? Yields are the returns investors earn for loaning money. Typically, the longer the bond’s term, the higher the yield investors seek, as they take on additional risk over a longer repayment period. Over the past year, yields for long-term government bonds have increased by 70–120 basis points in countries such as the United States, United Kingdom and Germany. What’s driving this rise? These nations are grappling with growing deficits, spending more than they earn and increasingly relying on external financing. This situation makes investors more hesitant to purchase long-term debt, as they are less certain of repayment. As demand decreases, the yield required by investors to assume this risk increases. The bond market’s response underscores the need for strategic fiscal management to restore investor confidence as these governments tackle financial challenges.

Investing in fixed income products is subject to certain risks, including interest rate, credit, inflation, call, prepayment and reinvestment risk. Any fixed income security sold or redeemed prior to maturity may be subject to substantial gain or loss.

node.text:
At the Private Bank, leadership means understanding the unique needs of the clients and communities we serve. Market Team Lead Danielle Wright and her experienced team work closely with individuals, families and institutions across Utah and Idaho to help them achieve their financial goals. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Salt Lake City team.

Earlier in the year, Wall Street was optimistic about U.S. growth, expecting deregulation and economic momentum to drive higher rates. Now, 10-year yields are climbing due to concerns over mounting deficits rather than growth expectations. 
 
Despite the resilience of the American economy, we anticipate a slowdown in 2025, with real GDP growth around 1%, followed by a rebound in 2026. The current policy mix—characterized by higher deficits, elevated long-term rates and a weaker dollar—challenges the longstanding dominance of U.S. exceptionalism. In response, the Federal Reserve is likely to gradually cut rates, potentially twice in both 2025 and 2026.
 
Equity markets can still find room to grow, but global diversification and portfolio resilience matter.

Diversification and asset allocation does not ensure a profit or protect against loss.

With global tariffs reaching a potential state of equilibrium, we can return to some regularly scheduled programming. Michael Cembalest, our Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, turns his analysis to the effects of corporate AI adoption on equity markets. As companies are increasingly focusing on AI over tariffs, he discusses the surge in AI investments, advancements in capabilities and the hunt for growth in AI-related revenues.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

From the potential impacts of Trump 2.0 to building resilient portfolios amid growing uncertainty, our 2025 #MidYearOutlook unveils essential insights to keep your wealth plan on track. Explore five key questions investors can use to guide their investment decision making. 

Tap the link in bio to read more in our 2025 Mid-Year Outlook report. #jpmorganprivatebank

Today, we recognize the countless sacrifices made by our military, veterans, first responders and their families. For more than a decade, we’ve worked with our Office of Military and Veterans Affairs to partner with Carry The Load, a national nonprofit that helps Americans remember the true meaning of #MemorialDay.

President Trump’s tax bill has passed the House, shifting focus from tariffs to deficits. As fiscal policies evolve, we explore strategies to protect portfolios from increasing deficits, including dollar diversification, gold and infrastructure investments. 

Tap the link in bio to discover how to build resilience in your portfolio in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.
In celebration of Asian and Pacific Islander (API) Heritage Month, we recognize and celebrate the cultures, stories and contributions of our #API colleagues across the firm. #APIHeritageMonth

Is your portfolio resilient in the face of growing risks? Cultivating a balanced mix of assets with low correlations is crucial for enhancing resilience and minimizing drawdowns. Regularly reviewing and rebalancing your portfolio ensures it aligns with your investment goals and risk tolerance. While Bitcoin may have a low correlation to traditional assets, its drawdowns underscore the volatility of cryptocurrency investments, which can significantly increase risk exposure. Similarly, gold also provides a low correlation to traditional assets. However, gold has the additional benefits of lower volatility than crypto, and is a stable hedge against geopolitical risks and dollar weakness. By proactively managing your investments and avoiding concentration in any single asset class, you can better navigate market fluctuations and may help achieve long-term growth. Diversification is key to building a portfolio that withstands current evolving market challenges.

Tap the link in bio to read more in our 2025 #MidYearOutlook.

Diversification and asset allocation does not ensure a profit or protect against loss. Investments in commodities may have greater volatility than investments in traditional securities, particularly if the instruments involve leverage. J.P. Morgan Securities LLC does not endorse, advise on, transmit, sell or transact in any type of virtual currency.

node.text:
We recently hosted the third annual Women’s Forum in Hong Kong, centered around the theme “The Artistry of Resilience.”

During a conversation with Lhakpa Sherpa, the world record holder for the most Mount Everest summits by a woman, she shared her inspiring story—from her early life and passion for the mountains to being named “The Mountain Queen.” She offered valuable insights on resilience and overcoming personal challenges to reach her own summit.

This was followed by an in-depth discussion with leaders in the art industry, including Dr. Charlotte Eyerman, Global Head of the JPMorganChase Corporate Art Collection, Elaine Kwok, Independent Cultural Strategist, and Mireya Lewin, Senior Vice President of Art Collections for the Jordan Schnitzer Family Foundation. They discussed their professional journeys in the art world and the importance of diversity among collectors, artists, curators and gallerists.

Tap the link in bio to read the full interview.

The 2025 Landowner Summit brought together J.P. Morgan Private Bank advisors and leaders with landowners and experts from Texas and abroad. The event featured panel discussions on Texas growth, land as an asset and wildfire prevention strategies. Attendees delved into ranch management best practices, succession planning for family-owned land and tech-driven monetization opportunities. 

Tap the link in bio to discover how our Private Bank team can assist you.

The release of the proposed reconciliation bill language marks a pivotal moment for U.S. fiscal policy. As tax cuts and spending adjustments take center stage, we explore the potential impacts on the economy and markets. 

Tap the link in bio to discover how these changes could create investment opportunities. #TopMarketTakeaways

node.text:
Embedded in the vibrant community of Pasadena, our bankers bring together local insight and the full strength of J.P. Morgan to support the people and institutions shaping the city. Whether guiding clients through complex financial scenarios or giving back through local initiatives, our commitment runs deep. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how we can help you get closer to your financial goals.

Global equity markets have staged an impressive recovery from the early April “Liberation Day” shock, underscoring their sensitivity to evolving trade policy. Despite this recovery, the looming threat of tariffs continues to cast a shadow over the economic landscape. As trade negotiations unfold, we anticipate ongoing volatility, especially with the summer’s tariff postponement ending in the midst of U.S. budget reconciliation and debt ceiling discussions. Recent progress in U.S.-China trade talks suggests movement toward a deal, but comprehensive agreements require time and trust. Diversifying geographically and across asset classes is crucial to ensuring portfolio resilience amid these ongoing uncertainties. By spreading investments across different regions and sectors, investors can create a smoother ride and capitalize on opportunities that arise from shifting global dynamics.

The #MiamiGP was a remarkable showcase of speed and precision. We were delighted to host our guests at the Paddock Club, experiencing the thrill of the race firsthand. Thank you to everyone involved in this exceptional partnership. Inspired by the drivers, we continue to push the boundaries of excellence.

In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we explore the “wait and see” theme as the market anticipates how hard data will respond to current economic uncertainties. We examine the resilience of domestic demand despite a Q1 GDP decline, the Federal Reserve’s cautious stance amid tariff risks, and the stability of trade and corporate sentiment indicators.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Oil prices have plummeted to a four-year low, dropping over 15% in April alone—the steepest decline since November 2021. What’s driving this downturn? It’s a classic case of supply and demand. OPEC+ (Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries) recently announced two large and unexpected output increases. With supply on the rise, markets are concerned that it will surpass demand, pushing prices further down. Adding to the pressure, investors are wary of reduced demand amid fears of a looming recession. If uncertainty continues to grow, and we do fall into a recession, consumers will likely cut back, especially on travel, which will in turn decrease oil demand and lead to lower prices. Lower oil prices can lead to reduced costs for consumers and businesses, potentially boosting economic activity, but they can also hurt oil-producing economies and industries.

At our 2025 Landowner Summit, advisors and leaders from J.P. Morgan Private Bank joined landowners and land management experts from across Texas and abroad to share insights and practical advice. The event featured engaging panel discussions on topics such as the impact of growth in Texas, the role of land as an asset class, and strategies for wildfire prevention and mitigation. Attendees also explored best practices in ranch management, succession planning for family-owned land and innovative monetization opportunities emerging from rapid technology advancements.

Tap the link in our bio to learn more about how our Private Bank team can help you.

In the latest instalment of My Cup of Tea—our Women & Wealth interview series—we speak to Cressida Myers, Head of Sustainable Portfolio Management.

Discussing her involvement with Girls Are Investors (GAIN), Cressida outlines the challenges associated with her role, the philosophy that underpins sustainable investing and how she balances her day-to-day finances. 

Tap the link in bio to read the full interview.

From tariffs to the budget deficit, President Trump’s first 100 days have reshaped the investment landscape. Our #TopMarketTakeaways offers strategies for navigating these changes, including diversification and global exposure. 

Tap the link in bio to discover how to position your portfolio for success.

We were delighted to welcome clients to our New York City headquarters for Alternative Investment Insights 2025 (AII). Attending a half-day of inspiring sessions, guests heard from our high-conviction managers, who discussed trends and opportunities in public and private markets. #AlternativeInvestmentInsights2025

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text: Congratulations to our newly promoted Managing Directors on their well-deserved promotions. These leaders have each shown exceptional dedication to our clients, and bring a wealth of experience and knowledge to their new roles. We look forward to seeing their future achievements as they continue to partner with clients on their financial journeys.

In 2025, the U.S. dollar has defied its traditional role as a safe haven currency. Historically, the dollar has been known to appreciate in both market downturns, when investors seek safety, and during economic upswings, driven by strong U.S. growth. However, this year has seen a surprising shift: The dollar has weakened in tandem with U.S. stocks and bonds—a rare occurrence that signals a shift in investor confidence in U.S. assets.
 
Investors may consider diversifying globally by investing in non-U.S. markets. Diversification and asset allocation does not ensure a profit or protect against loss. International investments may not be suitable for all investors. International investing involves a greater degree of risk and increased volatility. 

Tap the link in bio to take a look at our most recent article to go deeper.

Get ready to rev your engines. The #MiamiGP race weekend is here, and we’re thrilled to be back in the driver’s seat as a founding partner. From the adrenaline-pumping action on the track to the unforgettable experiences off it, this weekend promises to be a high-octane celebration of speed and innovation. Join us as we embrace the thrill of the race and the spirit of competition.

Amid market volatility, explore diverse investment opportunities: European equities with growth potential, tax-efficient municipal bonds and private equity secondaries for liquidity. 

Tap the link in bio to discover how these options can enhance your portfolio’s resilience and returns. #TopMarketTakeaways

Our yachting legacy dates back over 100 years to our founder John Pierpont Morgan. We are proud to continue this passion and be the Official Bank Partner for the Singapore Yachting Festival 2025—supporting a vibrant celebration of yachting, and engaging with the community from Singapore and across Asia.

Discover more in the link in our bio.

The U.S. dollar is losing some ground, while Treasury yields are on the rise, which is a bit different from what we’re used to seeing. This could be because investors are worried about things such as tariff issues, the Federal Reserve’s independence, and growing budget deficits. These concerns might be shaking up investor confidence both here and abroad. But even with these bumps in the road, the United States is still the biggest economy with the most liquid markets. It’s a good reminder of why spreading investments across various countries and asset classes is key to building strong portfolios, no matter what the future holds.

node.text: We kicked off the first installment of our global 2025 Emerging Family Leaders series in the city of Los Angeles. During this engaging two-day event, future decision makers and innovators immersed themselves in an array of cultural experiences and insightful panel discussions. #jpmorganprivatebank

With tariffs creating market turbulence, our #TopMarketTakeaways outlines three possible scenarios and their implications for investors. 

Tap the link in bio to learn how you can manage volatility and position your portfolio in this uncertain landscape.

The University of Michigan’s Consumer Sentiment Index, a key measure of consumer confidence indicating how optimistic or pessimistic consumers feel about the economy, dropped to 51, nearing historic lows. Historically, such low confidence levels have often preceded market rebounds. In May 1980, a sentiment reading of 52 led to a 19% S&P 500 increase over the next year. Similarly, November 2008’s sentiment reading of 55 saw a 22% rise only one year later. Markets often don’t move on good or bad news, but on whether the news was better or worse than expected. While past performance does not guarantee future returns, at such downtrodden levels, sentiment may offer the potential for an upside surprise.

We are excited to share highlights from our recent U.S. Private Bank Senior Leaders Conference held in Phoenix, Arizona. This three-day event brought senior leaders from across the U.S. Private Bank together for an inspiring and productive gathering. Attendees participated in a dynamic mix of general and breakout sessions designed to foster meaningful discussions and spark innovative ideas, as well as facilitate the exchange of collaborative solutions that will support our commitment to providing the highest levels of service to every client.

Volatility is creating both challenges and opportunities in the wake of new tariffs. From the impact on multinational corporations to the potential for policy responses, our five takeaways offer guidance on navigating this complex landscape. 

Tap the link in bio to explore our strategies for maintaining portfolio resilience in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

In today’s volatile stock market, understanding the path back to market highs is essential. This week’s chart illustrates the average annual return needed for the S&P 500 to reach its peak again. The recent sell-off, spurred by “Liberation Day” tariffs, has caused significant market disruptions, with the S&P 500 dropping over 10% last week. These tariffs exceeded market expectations, creating uncertainty and challenging the business models of multinational corporations. Our analysis suggests that the current tariff policy could negate the anticipated economic and earnings growth for the year.
 
The chart shows that a one-year recovery would require a 13% annual total return, while a longer timeline reduces the needed return to 6% over two years and 4% over three years, which emphasizes the importance of maintaining a long-term investment perspective.
 
During these times, focus on maintaining a diversified portfolio and staying informed about market developments. Whether it’s reducing risk, exploring structured notes or considering fixed income options, aligning your approach with your long-term goals is crucial. Focus on these elements to navigate the path back to market highs, turning volatility into an opportunity for growth.

node.text:
J.P. Morgan Private Bank is excited to announce its second year of partnership with @theauroraconcours. 

Championing the elegance and innovation of automobile craftsmanship, this exclusive event invites guests to explore Scandinavian luxury and a curated collection of the world’s most impressive vehicles—set amidst the coastal serenity of Sweden’s Båstad landscape.

Built for connoisseurs, it promises to be an unforgettable experience. 
 
Discover more at the link in bio.

The recent tariff announcement by President Trump has sent shockwaves through the markets, with S&P 500 and NASDAQ 100 futures experiencing steep declines. These tariffs, which are at the upper end of expectations, could significantly hinder U.S. economic growth and drive inflation higher. This week’s #TopMarketTakeaways spotlight provides an in-depth analysis of these changes and their potential impact on investment strategies.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

It was an underwhelming quarter for U.S. equities. The S&P 500 returned -4.6%, which was the worst start to a year since 2022. On the other hand, European and Chinese equities have been off to historically strong starts. The dichotomy has led to the largest quarterly underperformance of U.S. equities relative to global developed markets since 2002.
 
While we think U.S. equities can outperform global equities into year-end, the current performance of global equities highlights the importance of having diversified portfolios across geographies.

Three key risks from Washington are shaping market dynamics: looming tariffs, potential changes to municipal bond tax exemptions, and radical currency strategies. As these developments unfold, strategic asset allocation and portfolio resilience are crucial. 

Tap the link in bio to learn how to position your investments for stability in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

At Diane von Furstenberg’s InCharge event in New York City, we celebrated International Women’s Day and Women’s History Month with inspiring leaders from different walks of life. From thought-provoking panels to intimate conversations, the event explored what it truly means to be in charge and how we can continue to make an impact. At the Private Bank, we are committed to helping clients build wealth and shape their legacies. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Diverse Wealth Strategies Team.

Recent indicators have shown consumers are increasing their expectations for inflation. If consumers expect higher prices, they demand higher wages to keep up with the cost of living, and businesses raise prices to cover higher wage costs and maintain profit margins. As inflation continues to rise, expectations become even more de-anchored, leading to further wage and price increases. In that scenario, central banks may need to implement aggressive monetary policies, such as raising interest rates, to break the spiral and re-anchor inflation expectations.
 
While the University of Michigan’s consumer expectations indicate a rise in inflation, it’s crucial to note that other measures haven’t mirrored this increase. In fact, some have even shown a decline. For the Federal Reserve and markets, it’s important that long-term inflation expectations remain anchored near the Fed’s 2% target to help mitigate the risk of a wage spiral.
 
Keeping long-term inflation expectations anchored is crucial for investors aiming to achieve their financial goals, as it helps maintain economic stability and predictability. With inflation near target levels, central banks create a favorable environment for confident investment planning and growth.

Morgan’s global resources and local insight to empower individuals, families and businesses. From building wealth to preserving legacies, we’re here to help you reach your goals. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Salt Lake City team.

Navigating the current market requires understanding both the bull and bear perspectives. As risks evolve, strategic asset allocation remains key. Discover how to enhance your portfolio’s resilience and seize opportunities with our comprehensive analysis. #TopMarketTakeaways

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We’re proud to recognize #WomensHistoryMonth and celebrate women’s contributions to our society, reaffirming our longstanding commitment to support women and all communities in and out of the workplace.

In the world of investing, diversification is key, and this week’s chart highlights the power of bonds in providing stability during turbulent times. Since 1977, U.S. Aggregate Bonds have consistently delivered positive returns in years when the S&P 500 has faced declines, with the exception of 2022. This historical trend underscores the importance of including fixed income in your investment strategy to hedge against market downturns.
 
As we navigate the current market landscape, the S&P 500 has experienced a year-to-date decline of about 4%. However, U.S. and global core bonds have shown their resilience, both posting positive gains. This performance demonstrates how bonds can act as a hedge, helping to protect your portfolio from the full impact of stock market volatility. By balancing growth-oriented investments with the stability of fixed income, you can better weather the ups and downs of the market and work toward achieving your long-term financial goals.

Investing in fixed income products is subject to certain risks, including interest rate, credit, inflation, call, prepayment and reinvestment risk. Any fixed income security sold or redeemed prior to maturity may be subject to substantial gain or loss.

Arizona’s growth is opening up new opportunities, and we’re here to help clients make the most of them. Led by Noreen Bishop Hill, our integrated team of specialists across banking, lending, wealth planning and investing taps into their diverse strengths to create a seamless experience. By collaborating closely, we craft strategies tailored to the evolving needs of our clients to help them navigate complexity and embrace new possibilities. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Phoenix team.

Tariff tensions and uncertainty have led to a 10% pullback in the S&P 500, but history suggests there’s potential for a rebound. As we assess the market dynamics, consider how fixed income and diversification can add resilience to your portfolio.

Tap the link in bio to discover more in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

The U.S. economy has been grappling with heightened volatility and market declines. Following the post-election “Trump bump” in business optimism, today’s skyrocketing trade policy uncertainty is overshadowing confidence. The current market environment is different from Trump 1.0, where there was an 18-month gap before trade policy concerns caught up with optimism. Now, uncertainty leads the narrative. 

We acknowledge the elevated risks currently seen in the market, and advocate for adding resilience to portfolios through geographic and asset diversification.

Diversification and asset allocation does not ensure a profit or protect against loss.

While renewable electricity generation is increasing in the U.S. and many other countries, its impact on overall energy consumption remains elusive. This suggests that despite visible progress, the ultimate goal of comprehensive energy transformation may be further away than it seems. Many countries, including the U.S., face challenges in translating this growth into broader energy consumption. 

Michael Cembalest, Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, explores the paradox of renewable energy’s growth and more in the 15th Annual Eye on the Market Energy Paper titled “Heliocentrism: Objects May Be Further Than They Appear.”

Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

With tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China fluctuating, the market landscape is rapidly shifting, leading U.S. markets to their worst week since September. This week’s #TopMarketTakeaways delves into the effects on global markets, highlights the impact on key sectors, and offers strategic guidance on diversifying portfolios to navigate ongoing trade policy changes. Discover how to build resilience in your investments by tapping our link in bio.

Yesterday, the U.S. effective tariff rate increased significantly as a new series of tariffs came into effect: 25% on goods from Mexico and Canada (10% on Canadian energy), and an additional 10% on Chinese goods. These tariffs have pushed the U.S. effective tariff rate to its highest level since WWII, with China’s rate soaring to 34%. We currently expect a more moderate impact on the United States and a larger impact on the economies of Mexico and Canada. We estimate that these latest tariffs could result in a potential hit to U.S. growth of approximately 50–100 basis points and a boost to inflation of around 50–100 basis points. If additional tariffs are enacted, the impact may be even greater. However, the potential impact of the tariffs has a wide range, and is further influenced by variables such as substitution, currency fluctuations and retaliatory measures.

Just released: Many are optimistic about the energy transition, pointing to rapid growth in solar power and energy storage, but the reality is complex. Some prior industrial transitions were relatively quick as the new technology paid for itself and rewarded early adopters, but that’s not the case for renewables. In his 15th annual energy paper, Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy Michael Cembalest breaks down the recent surge in global solar capacity, as well as electrification, nuclear power, the Trump 2.0 energy policies, the Los Angeles fires, renewable jet fuel, and much more.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

At J.P. Morgan Private Bank, we provide insights and resources to help you succeed. My Cup of Tea, our Women & Wealth interview series, presents the viewpoints of leading women from across the firm. 
 
This time, we chat with Carlotta Saporito, our Head of Impact Investing, who shares her path to success, dispels myths about the practice of impact investing and describes her hopes for the future. 

Tap the link in bio to read the full interview.

This year, U.S. equities are underperforming as Europe and China rally due to sentiment and valuation shifts. This highlights the importance of diversification. Fixed income remains crucial, offering diversification from growth shocks and benefiting from higher yields. Explore more of the unexpected market dynamics in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways. 

Tap the link in bio for more.

While European and Asian equity markets have posted impressive gains this year, U.S. equities have lagged. This underperformance highlights the importance of diversification in investment portfolios. Relying solely on U.S. equities could mean missing out on potential growth opportunities in other markets. However, diversification isn’t about outperformance, it’s about reducing concentration risk and volatility by adding different drivers of return. Exposure to international markets can help investors manage risk and seize global opportunities.

Located in a beautiful Park Shore office, our Naples team is working hard to help a new generation of local residents achieve all they envision for their wealth. With a wide range of specialties, our Bankers have deep experience in advising individuals and families in this dynamic region. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Naples banking team.

With global armed conflict at an 80-year high, changes in defense spending are imminent. As Europe boosts military budgets and reassesses its energy reliance, we’ll likely see a greater focus on expanding both traditional and technological defense capabilities. #TopMarketTakeaways

Tap the link in bio to learn what these global shifts could mean.

This #BlackHistoryMonth, we’re proud to recognize our colleagues Grafton Ifill III, Jakeob Miranda, John McMichael and Tiffany S. Roland. As we celebrate the month, let’s come together to reflect on and work toward a future where everyone has a rightful “seat at the table.”

This earnings season, the term “tariffs” was mentioned a record 191 times during S&P 500 company earnings calls, breaking past highs from 2018 and 2019. 
 
The new administration has signed a slew of executive orders enacting and delaying tariffs. This has driven to the new spike we are seeing in company mentions. We expect continued announcements, including further details on tariffs regarding Mexico and China—the largest trading partners of the United States. 
 
For now, the stock market seems to be taking the tariff talk in stride. The S&P 500 is close to all-time highs, and European equities are higher by double digits this year. Risks remain, but investors’ reactions may mean they are hopeful that tariffs will prompt negotiations rather than an escalation into a trade war.

node.text:
Fresh from a thrilling victory in Sydney, J.P. Morgan is proud to announce our global partnership with Emirates GBR, the formidable SailGP team led by renowned Olympic sailor Sir Ben Ainslie.

SailGP integrates high-speed racing with sustainable, cutting edge-edge marine technology, where ambition and teamwork push the limits of what is possible on the water.

This new collaboration reflects our ongoing commitment to the sport—recognizing sailing as a platform for championing shared values of excellence and innovation. 

Tap the link in bio for more.

#paidsponsorship

This week, we’re spotlighting the ongoing developments in global trade: The United States has announced a 25% tariff on all steel and aluminum imports. Canada, a major supplier, is the most impacted by this move, with $11.2 billion in steel and $9.5 billion in aluminum exports to the United States in 2024. Other key players such as Mexico and China are also affected, but Canada stands out as the largest contributor to U.S. imports in these sectors.
 
Ongoing tariff uncertainty has caused a recent stir in markets, affecting supply chains and pricing for U.S. companies. This has led to lower growth expectations and increased inflation concerns, causing increased volatility in financial markets. While the long-term implications of these tariffs remain unclear, it’s crucial for investors to stay informed and consider diversifying their portfolios. We suggest focusing on dollar-denominated assets and exploring resilient investments such as gold, infrastructure and real estate to navigate volatility in markets.

In this week’s market update, we delve into the implications of recent geopolitical developments and their potential impact on your investment strategy. Read #TopMarketTakeaways to understand what these developments may mean for your portfolio. Tap the link in bio for more.

node.text:
David Frame, the U.S. Private Bank’s CEO, reflects on the past year and shares some of the biggest questions clients are asking in 2025. Our team is honored to serve some of the most sophisticated individuals and families in the country. We are committed to helping our clients achieve their goals and fostering the next generation of leaders.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The new U.S. administration is increasingly planning on using tariffs as a trade tool. In the United States, exports make up just ~7% of its GDP, much less than countries such as Canada (27%) and Mexico (33%). This means all else equal, the United States is less affected by tariffs compared to its trading partners. With one of the most open economies and the lowest average tariff rates globally, the United States is taking steps to prioritize national security. 
 
However, these tariffs create uncertainty for businesses across North America. For everyday investors, we believe this is a good time to focus on dollar assets and diversify with resilient options such as gold, infrastructure and real estate to protect against market fluctuations.

Celebrating over a decade of inspiration at the Sundance Film Festival, J.P. Morgan Private Bank hosted its 11th annual reception in Park City, Utah, with 200+ attendees, including industry leaders. Danni Wright, Managing Director and Head of the Utah & Idaho Private Bank, welcomed guests, leading into a chat with André Holland, star of Moonlight and producer of Love Brooklyn. Chelsea Crowder-Luke, Executive Director in the Los Angeles Private Bank Office, moderated as André shared insights on his creative journey, amplifying Black voices and the impact of streaming. It was a fantastic evening of networking and inspiration. #SundanceFilmFestival #jpmorganprivatebank

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

J.P. Morgan Private Bank remains steadfast in its commitment to supporting women in their financial endeavours and journeys.

Our Women & Wealth initiative aims to empower women by providing a comprehensive collection of resources, insights & guidance to confidently navigate their unique financial journeys and achieve their aspirations. 

In this instalment of My Cup of Tea—our associated interview series—we sit down with Rashmi Gupta, Senior Emerging Markets Portfolio Manager. Transforming political passions into a flourishing career, she shares thoughts on up-and-coming economies, the importance of impact investing and what advice she’d give her younger self.

Tap the link in bio to read the full interview.

DeepSeek’s breakthrough in artificial intelligence technology is shaking up the market. By challenging assumptions about AI power and hardware needs, DeepSeek’s model is reshaping the AI trade landscape. As efficiency increases, the focus shifts from hardware to productivity gains. In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, discover how this impacts investment strategies and what it means for the future of AI.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

This week, DeepSeek, a Chinese AI startup, unveiled its R1 model, an open reasoning large language model (LLM) that reportedly rivals leading models such as OpenAI’s o1. This announcement triggered a significant market reaction, particularly impacting technology stocks. Tech hardware and semiconductors were hit hardest, dragging their year-to-date returns, with declines of 6.5% and 8.6%, respectively. DeepSeek’s breakthrough challenges assumptions about U.S. dominance in AI, and has prompted a reevaluation within the tech sector. The drawdown is isolated to these specific sectors within tech. While the broader market remains resilient, investors should stay informed about potential shifts in market dynamics and consider the importance of portfolio diversification as the AI landscape evolves.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text: Harshika Patel, Chief Executive Officer of J.P. Morgan Private Bank in Asia, shares Happy Lunar New Year wishes and her favorite traditions.

In just the first few days of President Trump’s new administration, we’ve seen a flurry of executive orders, a surprising pause on tariffs and the launch of a major artificial intelligence initiative. From immigration to energy, these early moves are shaping market expectations. Discover how these developments could impact the labor market, energy prices and international trade in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

In an unexpected twist, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield has risen to highs of over 110 basis points since the Federal Reserve began cutting rates, a deviation from the historical trend of falling yields. Our chart highlights this anomaly by comparing the current trend with past Fed cutting cycles since the 1980s.
 
Two main factors drive this atypical increase: stronger growth expectations and heightened macroeconomic uncertainty. As investors anticipate robust growth, they demand higher yields, while global uncertainty adds complexity. This chart shows how today’s market dynamics differ from the past, offering insights for your investment strategy. With a stable labor market and lagging interest-rate-sensitive sectors, we believe the Fed won’t raise rates further, creating opportunities as yields remain high.

What does 2025 have in store for the markets and global dynamics? Michael Cembalest’s latest Eye on the Market Outlook offers his insights.
 
Tap the link in bio to read his Top Ten List to understand how they might impact your investment strategy.

Our presence in Phoenix reflects both a proud history and a bold vision for the future. Our new state-of-the-art office is just one of the many ways we’re striving to enhance the client experience and strengthen our ties to the community. Whether you’re planning for today or building for future generations, we’re here to help you achieve your goals. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Phoenix team.

J.P. Morgan Private Bank, along with the Commercial & Investment Bank, proudly co-hosted the 17th Annual Women in Healthcare event, in association with the 43rd Annual Healthcare Conference in San Francisco, the largest healthcare investment symposium in the industry. At a reception honoring women executives and investment professionals, attendees heard from featured speakers Jamie Dimon, Chairman and CEO of JPMorganChase, and Terry-Ann Burrell, Vice Chair of Investment Banking, Healthcare, Commercial & Investment Bank.

A more fair and peaceful society — this was the enduring dream of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. As we celebrate his legacy, take a moment to consider how we might embrace his message of freedom, fairness, justice and love to build a brighter future for all.

Why are 10-year U.S. Treasury yields rising even as the Federal Reserve cuts rates? In this week’s #TopMarktetTakeaways, our analysis points to robust growth expectations and macroeconomic uncertainty as the main drivers. With the U.S. economy exceeding forecasts and the labor market stabilizing, however, yields have limited room to rise further. Explore what this means for your investment strategy. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Will Trump 2.0 substantially alter the federal revenue mix? 

The 2025 Eye on the Market Outlook, The Alchemists, discusses the challenges of stagnant U.S. industrial production. By increasing tariffs and reducing income tax receipts—a revenue mix reminiscent of the 1930s—the incoming administration is suggesting a change to the federal revenue mix to revitalize domestic growth. Efforts to boost investment within the United States are also gaining traction. Discover the potential impacts of these policies in our comprehensive report.

Tap link in bio to learn more.

Dive into the details of U.S. exceptionalism in equity markets, where the largest 10 stocks are achieving unprecedented free cash flow margins. While these giants soar, the rest of the S&P 500 tells a different story, highlighting a bifurcated market landscape. Michael Cembalest, Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy, explores this and more in the 2025 Eye on the Market Outlook.
 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

For the first time since early 2023, the 2-year and 10-year Treasury yields have risen above the fed funds rate. After a prolonged period of an inverted yield curve and concerns about a potential recession, the yield curve appears to be returning to its “normal” order (upward sloping). 

Why have yields surged? There are many reasons, one of which is a stronger than anticipated labor market. On Friday, new employment data exceeded expectations, indicating robust job growth. This suggests that the economy may be structurally stronger, and could keep inflation elevated for longer than previous expectations. Longer-term bond yields have been rising to price in this possibility.
